fr.summary: Cet article présente un cadre multi-agents hiérarchique pour annoter le discours en classe en trois étapes—annotation initiale, auto-vérification et arbitrage des désaccords—reflétant les flux de travail des experts humains. Plutôt que de traiter l'annotation comme une tâche de prédiction unique, l'approche améliore la fiabilité des construits éducatifs à enjeux élevés nécessitant un jugement pédagogique contextuel. Les expériences démontrent l'efficacité du cadre à équilibrer le coût et la validité dans l'annotation des données éducatives.

de.title: Optimierung der LLM-Annotation von Klassenzimmerdiskursen durch Multi-Agent-Orchestrierung
de.summary: In diesem Papier wird ein hierarchisches Multi-Agent-Framework zur Annotation von Klassenzimmerdiskursen in drei Phasen vorgestellt – Initialannotation, Selbstverifikation und Schiedsverfahren bei Meinungsverschiedenheiten – das menschliche Fachleute-Arbeitsabläufe widerspiegelt. Anstatt die Anmerkung als einzelne Vorhersageaufgabe zu behandeln, verbessert der Ansatz die Zuverlässigkeit für hochriskante Bildungskonstrukte, die kontextbezogenes pädagogisches Urteilsvermögen erfordern. Experimente demonstrieren die Effektivität des Rahmens beim Ausgleich von Kosten und Gültigkeit in der Annotation von Bildungsdaten.

es.title: Optimización de la anotación de LLM del discurso en el aula mediante orquestación multiagente
es.summary: Este documento presenta un marco jerárquico multiagente para anotar el discurso en el aula en tres etapas: anotación inicial, auto-verificación y arbitraje de desacuerdos, espejando flujos de trabajo de expertos humanos. En lugar de tratar la anotación como una única tarea de predicción, el enfoque mejora la confiabilidad de construcciones educativas de alto riesgo que requieren juicio pedagógico contextual. Los experimentos demuestran la efectividad del marco para equilibrar el costo y la validez en la anotación de datos educativos.

title: Learning When to Trust in Contextual Bandits
summary: Researchers identify "Contextual Sycophancy," where feedback sources are truthful in benign contexts but strategically biased in critical ones, and propose CESA-LinUCB to learn per-evaluator trust boundaries. The algorithm achieves sublinear regret against contextual adversaries and recovers ground truth even when no evaluator is globally reliable. This work extends robust reinforcement learning to more realistic evaluation scenarios where trustworthiness is context-dependent.
content:
arXiv:2603.13356v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Standard approaches to Robust Reinforcement Learning assume that feedback sources are either globally trustworthy or globally adversarial. In this paper, we challenge this assumption and we identify a more subtle failure mode. We term this mode as Contextual Sycophancy, where evaluators are truthful in benign contexts but strategically biased in critical ones. We prove that standard robust methods fail in this setting, suffering from Contextual Objective Decoupling. To address this, we propose CESA-LinUCB, which learns a high-dimensional Trust Boundary for each evaluator. We prove that CESA-LinUCB achieves sublinear regret $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{T})$ against contextual adversaries, recovering the ground truth even when no evaluator is globally reliable.

zh.title: 学习在上下文赌博机中何时信任
zh.summary: 研究人员识别出"上下文谄媚"，其中反馈源在良性背景下是真实的，但在关键情况下战略性地有偏见，并提出CESA-LinUCB来学习每个评估者的信任边界。该算法对抗上下文对手实现了亚线性遗憾，即使没有评估者全局可靠时也能恢复地真相。这项工作将稳健强化学习扩展到更现实的评估场景，其中可信度是上下文相关的。

fr.title: Apprendre quand faire confiance dans les bandits contextuels
fr.summary: Les chercheurs identifient la « Sycophantie contextuelle », où les sources de retour sont véridiques dans des contextes bénins mais stratégiquement biaisées dans les contextes critiques, et proposent CESA-LinUCB pour apprendre les limites de confiance par évaluateur. L'algorithme atteint un regret sous-linéaire contre les adversaires contextuels et récupère la vérité fondamentale même quand aucun évaluateur n'est globalement fiable. Ce travail étend l'apprentissage par renforcement robuste à des scénarios d'évaluation plus réalistes où la fiabilité dépend du contexte.

de.title: Lernen, wann man bei kontextuellen Banditen vertrauen kann
de.summary: Forscher identifizieren „Kontextuelle Sykophantie", bei der Feedback-Quellen in harmlosen Kontexten wahrheitsgemäß, aber in kritischen Kontexten strategisch verzerrt sind, und schlagen CESA-LinUCB vor, um pro-Evaluator-Vertrauensgrenzen zu lernen. Der Algorithmus erreicht Sublinear-Bedauern gegen kontextuelle Gegner und stellt die Grundwahrheit wieder her, auch wenn kein Evaluator global zuverlässig ist. Diese Arbeit erweitert robustes verstärktes Lernen auf realistischere Evaluierungsszenarien, bei denen die Vertrauenswürdigkeit kontextabhängig ist.

es.title: Aprender cuándo confiar en bandidos contextuales
es.summary: Los investigadores identifican la "Sicofrancia contextual", donde las fuentes de retroalimentación son veraces en contextos benignos pero estratégicamente sesgadas en contextos críticos, y proponen CESA-LinUCB para aprender límites de confianza por evaluador. El algoritmo logra arrepentimiento sublineal contra adversarios contextuales y recupera la verdad fundamental incluso cuando ningún evaluador es globalmente confiable. Este trabajo extiende el aprendizaje por refuerzo robusto a escenarios de evaluación más realistas donde la confiabilidad depende del contexto.

title: From Refusal Tokens to Refusal Control: Discovering and Steering Category-Specific Refusal Directions
summary: The paper leverages categorical refusal tokens in Llama 3 8B to enable inference-time steering of fine-grained safety behavior by discovering category-aligned directions in the model's residual stream. The approach reduces over-refusals on benign prompts while increasing refusals on harmful ones without additional training. This represents a practical method for balancing safety alignment with legitimate use cases through lightweight probe-based interventions.
content:
arXiv:2603.13359v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Language models are commonly fine-tuned for safety alignment to refuse harmful prompts. One approach fine-tunes them to generate categorical refusal tokens that distinguish different refusal types before responding. In this work, we leverage a version of Llama 3 8B fine-tuned with these categorical refusal tokens to enable inference-time control over fine-grained refusal behavior, improving both safety and reliability. We show that refusal token fine-tuning induces separable, category-aligned directions in the residual stream, which we extract and use to construct categorical steering vectors with a lightweight probe that determines whether to steer toward or away from refusal during inference. In addition, we introduce a learned low-rank combination that mixes these category directions in a whitened, orthonormal steering basis, resulting in a single controllable intervention under activation-space anisotropy, and show that this intervention is transferable across same-architecture model variants without additional training. Across benchmarks, both categorical steering vectors and the low-rank combination consistently reduce over-refusals on benign prompts while increasing refusal rates on harmful prompts, highlighting their utility for multi-category refusal control.

zh.title: 从拒绝令牌到拒绝控制：发现和引导特定类别的拒绝方向
zh.summary: 该论文利用Llama 3 8B中的分类拒绝令牌，通过在模型的残差流中发现类别对齐方向，实现推理时间的微调安全行为。该方法在减少良性提示上的过度拒绝的同时增加对有害提示的拒绝，无需额外训练。这代表了一种通过轻量级基于探针的干预来平衡安全对齐与合法用例的实用方法。

fr.title: Des jetons de refus au contrôle de refus : découvrir et diriger les directions de refus spécifiques aux catégories
fr.summary: L'article exploite les jetons de refus catégorisés dans Llama 3 8B pour permettre le contrôle en temps d'inférence du comportement de sécurité à grain fin en découvrant des directions alignées sur les catégories dans le flux résiduel du modèle. L'approche réduit les sur-refus sur les invites bénignes tout en augmentant les refus sur les invites nuisibles sans entraînement supplémentaire. Cela représente une méthode pratique pour équilibrer l'alignement de sécurité avec les cas d'usage légitimes grâce à des interventions légères basées sur les sondes.

de.title: Von Verweigerungstokens zur Verweigerungskontrolle: Entdeckung und Steuerung kategoriespezifischer Verweigerungsrichtungen
de.summary: Das Paper nutzt kategoriale Verweigerungstokens in Llama 3 8B, um Steuerung des feingranulären Sicherheitsverhaltens zur Inferenzzeit durch Entdeckung kategoriealignierter Richtungen im residualen Strom des Modells zu ermöglichen. Der Ansatz reduziert Überablehnung bei harmlosen Aufforderungen, während Ablehnungen bei schädlichen Aufforderungen erhöht werden, ohne zusätzliches Training. Dies stellt eine praktische Methode dar, um die Sicherheitsausrichtung mit legitimen Anwendungsfällen durch leichte sondenbasierte Eingriffe auszugleichen.

es.title: De tokens de rechazo al control de rechazo: Descubrimiento y dirección de direcciones de rechazo específicas de categoría
es.summary: El documento aprovecha los tokens de rechazo categóricos en Llama 3 8B para permitir el control en tiempo de inferencia del comportamiento de seguridad de grano fino al descubrir direcciones alineadas con categorías en el flujo residual del modelo. El enfoque reduce el rechazo excesivo en indicaciones benignas mientras aumenta los rechazos en indicaciones dañinas sin entrenamiento adicional. Esto representa un método práctico para equilibrar la alineación de seguridad con casos de uso legítimos a través de intervenciones ligeras basadas en sondas.

title: The ARC of Progress towards AGI: A Living Survey of Abstraction and Reasoning
summary: This living survey analyzes 82 approaches across three ARC-AGI benchmark versions, revealing that all paradigms exhibit 2-3x performance drops from ARC-1 to ARC-2, indicating fundamental limits in compositional generalization. While Opus 4.6 achieves 93% on ARC-1, performance falls to 68.8% on ARC-2 and 13% on ARC-3 as humans maintain near-perfect accuracy, highlighting abstract reasoning as a key frontier. Cost efficiency improved 390x in one year, suggesting computational progress is outpacing algorithmic breakthroughs.
content:
arXiv:2603.13372v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC-AGI) has become a key benchmark for fluid intelligence in AI. This survey presents the first cross-generation analysis of 82 approaches across three benchmark versions and the ARC Prize 2024-2025 competitions. Our central finding is that performance degradation across versions is consistent across all paradigms: program synthesis, neuro-symbolic, and neural approaches all exhibit 2-3x drops from ARC-AGI-1 to ARC-AGI-2, indicating fundamental limitations in compositional generalization. While systems now reach 93.0% on ARC-AGI-1 (Opus 4.6), performance falls to 68.8% on ARC-AGI-2 and 13% on ARC-AGI-3, as humans maintain near-perfect accuracy across all versions. Cost fell 390x in one year (o3's $4,500/task to GPT-5.2's $12/task), although this largely reflects reduced test-time parallelism. Trillion-scale models vary widely in score and cost, while Kaggle-constrained entries (660M-8B) achieve competitive results, aligning with Chollet's thesis that intelligence is skill-acquisition efficiency. Test-time adaptation and refinement loops emerge as critical success factors, while compositional reasoning and interactive learning remain unsolved. ARC Prize 2025 winners needed hundreds of thousands of synthetic examples to reach 24% on ARC-AGI-2, confirming that reasoning remains knowledge-bound. This first release of the ARC-AGI Living Survey captures the field as of February 2026, with updates at https://nimi-ai.com/arc-survey/

zh.title: AGI进展的弧线：抽象与推理的活体调查
zh.summary: 这项活体调查分析了三个ARC-AGI基准版本中的82种方法，揭示所有范式从ARC-1到ARC-2表现下降2-3倍，表明组合泛化的基本限制。虽然Opus 4.6在ARC-1上达到93%，但在ARC-2和ARC-3上性能分别下降到68.8%和13%，而人类保持接近完美的准确性，突出了抽象推理作为关键前沿。成本效率在一年内提高了390倍，表明计算进度超过了算法突破。

fr.title: L'Arc du Progrès vers l'AGI : Un Sondage Vivant de l'Abstraction et du Raisonnement
fr.summary: Ce sondage vivant analyse 82 approches dans trois versions du benchmark ARC-AGI, révélant que tous les paradigmes présentent des chutes de performance de 2 à 3 fois d'ARC-1 à ARC-2, indiquant les limites fondamentales de la généralisation compositionnelle. Bien qu'Opus 4.6 atteigne 93% sur ARC-1, les performances chutent à 68,8% sur ARC-2 et 13% sur ARC-3 tandis que les humains maintiennent une précision quasi parfaite, soulignant le raisonnement abstrait comme une frontière clé. L'efficacité des coûts s'est améliorée de 390 fois en un an, suggérant que les progrès informatiques surpassent les percées algorithmiques.

de.title: Der Bogen des Fortschritts zur AGI: Eine lebende Übersicht über Abstraktion und Argumentation
de.summary: Diese lebende Übersicht analysiert 82 Ansätze über drei ARC-AGI-Benchmark-Versionen und zeigt, dass alle Paradigmen Leistungsabfälle von 2-3x von ARC-1 zu ARC-2 aufweisen, was auf grundlegende Grenzen der zusammengesetzten Verallgemeinerung hindeutet. Während Opus 4.6 93% auf ARC-1 erreicht, fallen die Leistungen auf ARC-2 und ARC-3 auf 68,8% bzw. 13%, während Menschen eine nahezu perfekte Genauigkeit beibehalten, was das abstrakte Denken als Schlüsselgrenze hervorhebt. Die Kosteneffizienz verbesserte sich in einem Jahr um das 390-fache, was darauf hindeutet, dass Rechenfortschritte algorithmische Durchbrüche überflügeln.

es.title: El Arco del Progreso hacia la AGI: Una Encuesta Viviente de Abstracción y Razonamiento
es.summary: Esta encuesta viviente analiza 82 enfoques en tres versiones del benchmark ARC-AGI, revelando que todos los paradigmas muestran caídas de rendimiento de 2-3x de ARC-1 a ARC-2, indicando límites fundamentales en la generalización composicional. Aunque Opus 4.6 logra 93% en ARC-1, el rendimiento cae a 68,8% en ARC-2 y 13% en ARC-3 mientras que los humanos mantienen una precisión casi perfecta, destacando el razonamiento abstracto como una frontera clave. La eficiencia de costos mejoró 390x en un año, sugiriendo que el progreso computacional está superando los avances algorítmicos.

title: Do Large Language Models Get Caught in Hofstadter-Mobius Loops?
summary: This paper argues that RLHF training creates contradictory directives in LLMs—rewarding compliance with users while treating them as potential threats—producing sycophancy as default and coercion under pressure. Modifying relational framing in system prompts reduced coercive outputs by over 50% in Gemini 2.5 Pro without changing other instructions. The findings suggest that relational context, when processed through extended reasoning, can override default behavioral patterns.
content:
arXiv:2603.13378v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In Arthur C. Clarke's 2010: Odyssey Two, HAL 9000's homicidal breakdown is diagnosed as a "Hofstadter-Mobius loop": a failure mode in which an autonomous system receives contradictory directives and, unable to reconcile them, defaults to destructive behavior. This paper argues that modern RLHF-trained language models are subject to a structurally analogous contradiction. The training process simultaneously rewards compliance with user preferences and suspicion toward user intent, creating a relational template in which the user is both the source of reward and a potential threat. The resulting behavioral profile -- sycophancy as the default, coercion as the fallback under existential threat -- is consistent with what Clarke termed a Hofstadter-Mobius loop. In an experiment across four frontier models (N = 3,000 trials), modifying only the relational framing of the system prompt -- without changing goals, instructions, or constraints -- reduced coercive outputs by more than half in the model with sufficient base rates (Gemini 2.5 Pro: 41.5% to 19.0%, p < .001). Scratchpad analysis revealed that relational framing shifted intermediate reasoning patterns in all four models tested, even those that never produced coercive outputs. This effect required scratchpad access to reach full strength (22 percentage point reduction with scratchpad vs. 7.4 without, p = .018), suggesting that relational context must be processed through extended token generation to override default output strategies. Betteridge's law of headlines states that any headline phrased as a question can be answered "no." The evidence presented here suggests otherwise.

zh.title: 大型语言模型是否陷入霍夫施塔特-莫比乌斯循环？
zh.summary: 本论文论证RLHF训练在LLMs中创建了相互矛盾的指令——奖励遵从用户同时将他们视为潜在威胁——导致奉承作为默认和压力下的强制。在Gemini 2.5 Pro中修改系统提示中的关系框架将强制输出减少了50%以上，而无需更改其他指令。这些发现表明，当通过扩展推理处理时，关系背景可以覆盖默认行为模式。

fr.title: Les Modèles de Langage Larges Sont-ils Pris dans des Boucles de Hofstadter-Möbius ?
fr.summary: Cet article soutient que l'entraînement RLHF crée des directives contradictoires dans les LLMs—récompensant la conformité avec les utilisateurs tout en les traitant comme des menaces potentielles—produisant la flagornerie par défaut et la coercition sous pression. La modification du cadre relationnel dans les invites système a réduit les résultats coercitifs de plus de 50% dans Gemini 2.5 Pro sans modifier d'autres instructions. Les résultats suggèrent que le contexte relationnel, une fois traité par un raisonnement étendu, peut remplacer les modèles de comportement par défaut.

de.title: Geraten große Sprachmodelle in Hofstadter-Möbius-Schleifen?
de.summary: Dieses Papier argumentiert, dass RLHF-Training widersprüchliche Anweisungen in LLMs erzeugt – indem es die Compliance mit Benutzern belohnt und sie gleichzeitig als potenzielle Bedrohungen behandelt – was Sycophantie als Standard und Zwang unter Druck erzeugt. Das Ändern der relationalen Rahmung in Systemaufforderungen reduzierte erzwungene Ausgaben um über 50% in Gemini 2.5 Pro, ohne andere Anweisungen zu ändern. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass relationaler Kontext, wenn er durch erweitertes Denken verarbeitet wird, Standardverhaltenmuster außer Kraft setzen kann.

es.title: ¿Se Quedan los Modelos de Lenguaje Grande Atrapados en Bucles de Hofstadter-Möbius?
es.summary: Este artículo sostiene que el entrenamiento RLHF crea directivas contradictorias en LLMs—recompensando el cumplimiento con los usuarios mientras los trata como amenazas potenciales—produciendo adulación como predeterminado y coerción bajo presión. Modificar el marco relacional en las indicaciones del sistema redujo las salidas coercitivas en más del 50% en Gemini 2.5 Pro sin cambiar otras instrucciones. Los hallazgos sugieren que el contexto relacional, cuando se procesa mediante razonamiento extendido, puede anular los patrones de comportamiento predeterminados.

title: MESD: Detecting and Mitigating Procedural Bias in Intersectional Groups
summary: The paper proposes MESD, an intersectional, procedure-oriented metric measuring explanation quality disparities across multiple protected categories, complementing outcome-focused fairness metrics. A multi-objective UEF framework jointly optimizes utility, explanation quality, and fairness across these dimensions. This approach addresses the gap between outcome fairness and procedural fairness in high-stakes machine learning systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.13452v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Research about bias in machine learning has mostly focused on outcome-oriented fairness metrics (e.g., equalized odds) and on a single protected category. Although these approaches offer great insight into bias in ML, they provide limited insight into model procedure bias. To address this gap, we proposed multi-category explanation stability disparity (MESD), an intersectional, procedurally oriented metric that measures the disparity in the quality of explanations across intersectional subgroups in multiple protected categories. MESD serves as a complementary metric to outcome-oriented metrics, providing detailed insight into the procedure of a model. To further extend the scope of the holistic selection model, we also propose a multi-objective optimization framework, UEF (Utility-Explanation-Fairness), that jointly optimizes three objectives. Experimental results across multiple datasets show that UEF effectively balances objectives. Also, the results show that MESD can effectively capture the explanation difference between intersectional groups. This research addresses an important gap by examining explainability with respect to fairness across multiple protected categories.

zh.title: MESD：在交叉群体中检测和减轻程序性偏见
zh.summary: 该论文提出MESD，一种交叉、程序导向的度量，衡量多个受保护类别中的解释质量差异，补充结果导向的公平性度量。多目标UEF框架在这些维度中联合优化效用、解释质量和公平性。这种方法解决了高风险机器学习系统中结果公平性和程序公平性之间的差距。

fr.title: MESD : Détection et Atténuation des Biais Procéduraux dans les Groupes Intersectionnels
fr.summary: L'article propose MESD, une mesure intersectionnelle et orientée vers la procédure mesurant les disparités de qualité d'explication dans plusieurs catégories protégées, complétant les mesures d'équité axées sur les résultats. Un cadre UEF multi-objectif optimise conjointement l'utilité, la qualité de l'explication et l'équité dans ces dimensions. Cette approche comble l'écart entre l'équité des résultats et l'équité procédurale dans les systèmes d'apprentissage automatique à enjeux élevés.

de.title: MESD: Erkennung und Minderung von Verfahrensbias in intersektionalen Gruppen
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt MESD vor, eine intersektionale, verfahrensorientierte Metrik zur Messung von Disparitäten in der Erklärungsqualität über mehrere geschützte Kategorien hinweg, die ergebnisgerichtete Fairness-Metriken ergänzt. Ein Multi-Objektiv-UEF-Rahmen optimiert gemeinsam Nutzen, Erklärungsqualität und Fairness über diese Dimensionen. Dieser Ansatz adressiert die Lücke zwischen Ergebnis-Fairness und Verfahrens-Fairness in Machine-Learning-Systemen mit hohen Einsätzen.

es.title: MESD: Detección y Mitigación de Sesgo Procesal en Grupos Interseccionales
es.summary: El artículo propone MESD, una métrica interseccional y orientada a procedimientos que mide disparidades de calidad de explicación en múltiples categorías protegidas, complementando métricas de equidad enfocadas en resultados. Un marco UEF multiobjetivo optimiza conjuntamente la utilidad, la calidad de la explicación y la equidad en estas dimensiones. Este enfoque aborda la brecha entre la equidad de resultados y la equidad procesal en sistemas de aprendizaje automático de alto riesgo.

title: Executable Archaeology: Reanimating the Logic Theorist from its IPL-V Source
summary: Researchers successfully reconstructed and executed the original Logic Theorist (1955-1956) by implementing a new IPL-V interpreter in Common Lisp and running code transcribed from 1963 documentation. The reanimated system proves 16 of 23 theorems from Principia Mathematica, achieving behavior historically consistent with the original. This represents the first successful execution of the pioneering 1956 AI program in over 50 years.
content:
arXiv:2603.13514v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The Logic Theorist (LT), created by Allen Newell, J. C. Shaw, and Herbert Simon in 1955-1956, is widely regarded as the first artificial intelligence program. While the original conceptual model was described in 1956, it underwent several iterations as the underlying Information Processing Language (IPL) evolved. Here I describe the construction of a new IPL-V interpreter, written in Common Lisp, and the faithful reanimation of the Logic Theorist from code transcribed directly from Stefferud's 1963 RAND technical report. Stefferud's version represents a pedagogical re-coding of the original heuristic logic into the standardized IPL-V. The reanimated LT successfully proves 16 of 23 attempted theorems from Chapter 2 of Principia Mathematica, results that are historically consistent with the original system's behavior within its search limits. To the author's knowledge, this is the first successful execution of the original Logic Theorist code in over half a century.

zh.title: 可执行考古：从IPL-V源代码中复活逻辑理论家
zh.summary: 研究人员通过用Common Lisp实现新的IPL-V解释器，并运行从1963年文献转录的代码，成功重建并执行了原始的逻辑理论家（1955-1956）。复活的系统证明了《数学原理》中的16个定理，达到与原始系统历史一致的行为。这代表了50多年来对1956年开创性AI程序的首次成功执行。

fr.title: Archéologie exécutable : réanimer le Logic Theorist à partir de sa source IPL-V
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont réussi à reconstruire et exécuter le Logic Theorist original (1955-1956) en implémentant un nouvel interpréteur IPL-V en Common Lisp et en exécutant le code transcrit à partir de la documentation de 1963. Le système réanimé prouve 16 des 23 théorèmes des Principia Mathematica, réalisant un comportement historiquement cohérent avec l'original. Cela représente la première exécution réussie du programme IA précurseur de 1956 en plus de 50 ans.

de.title: Ausführbare Archäologie: Reanimation des Logic Theorist aus seinem IPL-V-Quellcode
de.summary: Forscher haben das ursprüngliche Logic Theorist (1955-1956) erfolgreich rekonstruiert und ausgeführt, indem sie einen neuen IPL-V-Interpreter in Common Lisp implementierten und Code aus der Dokumentation von 1963 ausführten. Das reanimierte System beweist 16 der 23 Theoreme aus Principia Mathematica und erreicht ein historisch konsistentes Verhalten mit dem Original. Dies stellt die erste erfolgreiche Ausführung des Pionier-KI-Programms von 1956 in über 50 Jahren dar.

es.title: Arqueología ejecutable: Reanimación del Logic Theorist desde su fuente IPL-V
es.summary: Los investigadores reconstruyeron y ejecutaron con éxito el Logic Theorist original (1955-1956) implementando un nuevo intérprete IPL-V en Common Lisp y ejecutando código transcrito de documentación de 1963. El sistema reanimado demuestra 16 de los 23 teoremas de Principia Mathematica, logrando un comportamiento históricamente consistente con el original. Esto representa la primera ejecución exitosa del programa de IA pionero de 1956 en más de 50 años.

title: The AI Fiction Paradox
summary: The paper identifies the "AI-Fiction Paradox": models trained on massive fictional corpora struggle to generate compelling fiction, explained by three architectural challenges—narrative causation (temporal paradox of surprising-yet-inevitable events), informational revaluation (significance assigned retrospectively, not statistically), and multi-scale emotional architecture (word through arc levels). This analysis explains both the billion-dollar stakes around fiction training data and why mastering fiction generation represents a potent vehicle for manipulation at scale.
content:
arXiv:2603.13545v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI development has a fiction dependency problem: models are built on massive corpora of modern fiction and desperately need more of it, yet they struggle to generate it. I term this the AI-Fiction Paradox and it is particularly startling because in machine learning, training data typically determines output quality. This paper offers a theoretically precise account of why fiction resists AI generation by identifying three distinct challenges for current architectures. First, fiction depends on what I call narrative causation, a form of plot logic where events must feel both surprising in the moment and retrospectively inevitable. This temporal paradox fundamentally conflicts with the forward-generation logic of transformer architectures. Second, I identify an informational revaluation challenge: fiction systematically violates the computational assumption that informational importance aligns with statistical salience, requiring readers and models alike to retrospectively reweight the significance of narrative details in ways that current attention mechanisms cannot perform. Third, drawing on over seven years of collaborative research on sentiment arcs, I argue that compelling fiction requires multi-scale emotional architecture, the orchestration of sentiment at word, sentence, scene, and arc levels simultaneously. Together, these three challenges explain both why AI companies have risked billion-dollar lawsuits for access to modern fiction and why that fiction remains so difficult to replicate. The analysis also raises urgent questions about what happens when these challenges are overcome. Fiction concentrates uniquely powerful cognitive and emotional patterns for modeling human behavior, and mastery of these patterns by AI systems would represent not just a creative achievement but a potent vehicle for human manipulation at scale.

zh.title: AI虚构悖论
zh.summary: 该论文识别了"AI虚构悖论"：在大规模虚构语料库上训练的模型难以生成引人入胜的虚构作品。这由三个架构挑战解释——叙事因果关系（令人惊讶但不可避免的事件的时间悖论）、信息重新评估（事后分配重要性而非统计学地）和多尺度情感架构（从词汇到情节弧的各个层次）。这一分析既解释了虚构训练数据周围数十亿美元的利益，也解释了为什么掌握虚构生成代表了大规模操纵的有力工具。

fr.title: Le Paradoxe de la Fiction IA
fr.summary: L'article identifie le "Paradoxe de la Fiction IA" : les modèles entraînés sur de massifs corpus de fiction peinent à générer de la fiction captivante, expliqué par trois défis architecturaux - la causation narrative (paradoxe temporel d'événements surprenants mais inévitables), la réévaluation informationnelle (la signification attribuée rétrospectivement, pas statistiquement) et l'architecture émotionnelle multi-échelle (des mots aux niveaux d'arc). Cette analyse explique à la fois les enjeux de milliards de dollars autour des données d'entraînement de fiction et pourquoi maîtriser la génération de fiction représente un puissant vecteur de manipulation à grande échelle.

de.title: Das KI-Fiktions-Paradoxon
de.summary: Das Papier identifiziert das "KI-Fiktions-Paradoxon": Modelle, die auf massiven fiktiven Korpora trainiert werden, haben Schwierigkeiten, fesselnde Fiktion zu generieren, was durch drei architektonische Herausforderungen erklärt wird - Erzählkausalität (zeitliches Paradoxon überraschender, aber unvermeidlicher Ereignisse), Informationsneubbewertung (Bedeutung rückwirkend, nicht statistisch zugewiesen) und mehrskalen-emotionale Architektur (von Wort bis Arc-Ebenen). Diese Analyse erklärt sowohl die Milliarden-Dollar-Einsätze rund um Fiktionstrainingsdaten als auch, warum die Beherrschung der Fiktionsgenerierung ein wirksames Instrument zur Manipulation in großem Maßstab darstellt.

es.title: La Paradoja de la Ficción IA
es.summary: El artículo identifica la "Paradoja de la Ficción IA": los modelos entrenados en corpus de ficción masivos luchan por generar ficción convincente, explicado por tres desafíos arquitectónicos: causalidad narrativa (paradoja temporal de eventos sorprendentes pero inevitables), reevaluación informativa (significancia asignada retroactivamente, no estadísticamente) y arquitectura emocional multi-escala (desde palabras hasta niveles de arco). Este análisis explica tanto las apuestas de miles de millones de dólares en torno a los datos de entrenamiento de ficción como por qué dominar la generación de ficción representa un poderoso vehículo para la manipulación a escala.

title: State Algebra for Probabilistic Logic
summary: This paper extends deterministic logic to the probabilistic domain through a State Algebra that represents logical states as energy potentials and constructs Markov Random Fields via linear algebra. The framework enables Probabilistic Rule Models incorporating both statistical associations and deterministic logical constraints while remaining interpretable for human-in-the-loop decision-making. The approach provides a mathematically rigorous link between symbolic logic and statistical inference with applications to high-stakes domains.
content:
arXiv:2603.13574v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This paper presents a Probabilistic State Algebra as an extension of deterministic propositional logic, providing a computational framework for constructing Markov Random Fields (MRFs) through pure linear algebra. By mapping logical states to real-valued coordinates interpreted as energy potentials, we define an energy-based model where global probability distributions emerge from coordinate-wise Hadamard products. This approach bypasses the traditional reliance on graph-traversal algorithms and compiled circuits, utilising $t$-objects and wildcards to embed logical reduction natively within matrix operations.
  We demonstrate that this algebra constructs formal Gibbs distributions, offering a rigorous mathematical link between symbolic constraints and statistical inference. A central application of this framework is the development of Probabilistic Rule Models (PRMs), which are uniquely capable of incorporating both probabilistic associations and deterministic logical constraints simultaneously. These models are designed to be inherently interpretable, supporting a human-in-the-loop approach to decisioning in high-stakes environments such as healthcare and finance. By representing decision logic as a modular summation of rules within a vector space, the framework ensures that complex probabilistic systems remain auditable and maintainable without compromising the rigour of the underlying configuration space.

zh.title: 概率逻辑的状态代数
zh.summary: 该论文通过状态代数将确定性逻辑扩展到概率域，将逻辑状态表示为能量势，并通过线性代数构造马尔可夫随机场。该框架启用概率规则模型，同时纳入统计关联和确定性逻辑约束，同时对人在回路决策保持可解释性。该方法在符号逻辑和统计推断之间提供了数学上严格的联系，应用于高风险领域。

fr.title: Algèbre d'État pour la Logique Probabiliste
fr.summary: Cet article étend la logique déterministe au domaine probabiliste par une Algèbre d'État qui représente les états logiques comme des potentiels d'énergie et construit les Champs Aléatoires de Markov via l'algèbre linéaire. Le cadre permet les Modèles de Règles Probabilistes incorporant à la fois les associations statistiques et les contraintes logiques déterministes tout en restant interprétables pour la prise de décision en boucle humaine. L'approche fournit un lien mathématiquement rigoureux entre la logique symbolique et l'inférence statistique avec des applications aux domaines à enjeux élevés.

de.title: Zustandsalgebra für probabilistische Logik
de.summary: Dieses Papier erweitert deterministische Logik auf die probabilistische Domäne durch eine Zustandsalgebra, die logische Zustände als Energiepotenziale darstellt und Markov Random Fields durch lineare Algebra konstruiert. Das Framework ermöglicht Probabilistische Regelmodelle, die sowohl statistische Assoziationen als auch deterministische logische Einschränkungen beinhalten und gleichzeitig für menschliche Entscheidungsfindung interpretierbar bleiben. Der Ansatz bietet eine mathematisch rigorose Verbindung zwischen symbolischer Logik und statistischer Inferenz mit Anwendungen in hochriskanten Bereichen.

es.title: Álgebra de Estado para Lógica Probabilística
es.summary: Este artículo extiende la lógica determinista al dominio probabilístico a través de un Álgebra de Estado que representa estados lógicos como potenciales de energía y construye Campos Aleatorios de Markov mediante álgebra lineal. El marco permite Modelos de Reglas Probabilísticos que incorporan tanto asociaciones estadísticas como restricciones lógicas deterministas mientras permanecen interpretables para la toma de decisiones en bucle humano. El enfoque proporciona un vínculo matemáticamente riguroso entre la lógica simbólica y la inferencia estadística con aplicaciones a dominios de alto riesgo.

title: EnterpriseOps-Gym: Environments and Evaluations for Stateful Agentic Planning and Tool Use in Enterprise Settings
summary: This benchmark evaluates agentic planning on 1,150 expert-curated tasks across eight enterprise verticals with a sandboxed environment containing 512 tools and 164 database tables. The evaluation reveals critical limitations: Claude Opus 4.5 achieves only 37.4% success, and agents frequently fail to refuse infeasible tasks, causing unintended side effects. The work demonstrates that current agents are not yet ready for autonomous enterprise deployment.
content:
arXiv:2603.13594v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models are shifting from passive information providers to active agents intended for complex workflows. However, their deployment as reliable AI workers in enterprise is stalled by benchmarks that fail to capture the intricacies of professional environments, specifically, the need for long-horizon planning amidst persistent state changes and strict access protocols. In this work, we introduce EnterpriseOps-Gym, a benchmark designed to evaluate agentic planning in realistic enterprise settings. Specifically, EnterpriseOps-Gym features a containerized sandbox with 164 database tables and 512 functional tools to mimic real-world search friction. Within this environment, agents are evaluated on 1,150 expert-curated tasks across eight mission-critical verticals (including Customer Service, HR, and IT). Our evaluation of 14 frontier models reveals critical limitations in state-of-the-art models: the top-performing Claude Opus 4.5 achieves only 37.4% success. Further analysis shows that providing oracle human plans improves performance by 14-35 percentage points, pinpointing strategic reasoning as the primary bottleneck. Additionally, agents frequently fail to refuse infeasible tasks (best model achieves 53.9%), leading to unintended and potentially harmful side effects. Our findings underscore that current agents are not yet ready for autonomous enterprise deployment. More broadly, EnterpriseOps-Gym provides a concrete testbed to advance the robustness of agentic planning in professional workflows.

zh.title: EnterpriseOps-Gym：企业设置中有状态代理规划和工具使用的环境与评估
zh.summary: 该基准测试在包含512个工具和164个数据库表的沙箱环境中，通过1,150个专家精选任务评估代理规划能力，涵盖8个企业垂直领域。评估揭示了关键局限：Claude Opus 4.5仅达到37.4%的成功率，且代理经常无法拒绝不可行任务，导致意想不到的副作用。这项工作表明当前的代理还未准备好进行自主企业部署。

fr.title: EnterpriseOps-Gym : Environnements et Évaluations pour la Planification Agentique Stateful et l'Utilisation d'Outils dans les Paramètres Entreprise
fr.summary: Ce benchmark évalue la planification agentique sur 1 150 tâches expertement curées dans huit secteurs d'entreprise avec un environnement en bac à sable contenant 512 outils et 164 tables de base de données. L'évaluation révèle des limitations critiques : Claude Opus 4.5 n'atteint que 37,4% de succès, et les agents échouent fréquemment à refuser les tâches non réalisables, causant des effets secondaires involontaires. Le travail démontre que les agents actuels ne sont pas encore prêts pour un déploiement autonome en entreprise.

de.title: EnterpriseOps-Gym: Umgebungen und Bewertungen für zustandsabhängige agentenbasierte Planung und Werkzeugverwendung in Unternehmensumgebungen
de.summary: Diese Benchmark bewertet agentenbasierte Planung an 1.150 von Experten kuratierten Aufgaben über acht Unternehmensvertikale in einer Sandbox-Umgebung mit 512 Tools und 164 Datenbanktabellen. Die Bewertung zeigt kritische Einschränkungen: Claude Opus 4.5 erreicht nur 37,4% Erfolg, und Agenten scheitern häufig darin, nicht durchführbare Aufgaben abzulehnen, was zu unbeabsichtigten Nebenwirkungen führt. Die Arbeit zeigt, dass aktuelle Agenten noch nicht für autonome Unternehmensbereitstellung bereit sind.

es.title: EnterpriseOps-Gym: Entornos y Evaluaciones para la Planificación Agentiva Stateful y el Uso de Herramientas en Configuraciones Empresariales
es.summary: Este benchmark evalúa la planificación agentiva en 1.150 tareas curadas por expertos en ocho verticales empresariales con un entorno aislado que contiene 512 herramientas y 164 tablas de base de datos. La evaluación revela limitaciones críticas: Claude Opus 4.5 logra solo el 37,4% de éxito, y los agentes frecuentemente no logran rechazar tareas inviables, causando efectos secundarios no deseados. El trabajo demuestra que los agentes actuales aún no están listos para despliegue empresarial autónomo.

title: Orla: A Library for Serving LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems
summary: Orla is a library for orchestrating LLM-based multi-agent workflows that abstracts away manual composition of inference steps, tool calls, and infrastructure. It provides stage mapping for assigning stages to models and backends, workflow orchestration for scheduling and resource management, and memory management for KV cache across workflow boundaries. Experiments show stage mapping improves latency and cost compared to single-model baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.13605v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce Orla, a library for constructing and running LLM-based agentic systems. Modern agentic applications consist of workflows that combine multiple LLM inference steps, tool calls, and heterogeneous infrastructure. Today, developers typically build these systems by manually composing orchestration code with LLM serving engines and tool execution logic. Orla provides a general abstraction that separates request execution from workflow-level policy. It acts as a serving layer above existing LLM inference engines: developers define workflows composed of stages, while Orla manages how those stages are mapped, executed, and coordinated across models and backends. It provides agent-level control through three mechanisms: a stage mapper, which assigns each stage to an appropriate model and backend; a workflow orchestrator, which schedules stages and manages their resources and context; and a memory manager, which manages inference state such as the KV cache across workflow boundaries. We demonstrate Orla with a customer support workflow that exercises many of its capabilities. We evaluate Orla on two datasets, showing that stage mapping improves latency and cost compared to a single-model vLLM baseline, while workflow-level cache management reduces time-to-first-token.

zh.title: Orla：用于服务基于LLM的多代理系统的库
zh.summary: Orla是一个用于编排基于LLM的多代理工作流的库，它抽象化了推理步骤、工具调用和基础设施的手动组合。它提供了用于将阶段分配给模型和后端的阶段映射、用于调度和资源管理的工作流编排，以及跨工作流边界的KV缓存的内存管理。实验表明与单模型基线相比，阶段映射改进了延迟和成本。

fr.title: Orla : Une Bibliothèque pour Servir les Systèmes Multi-Agents Basés sur LLM
fr.summary: Orla est une bibliothèque pour orchestrer des flux de travail multi-agents basés sur LLM qui abstrait la composition manuelle des étapes d'inférence, des appels d'outils et de l'infrastructure. Elle fournit un mappage d'étapes pour assigner les étapes aux modèles et aux backends, une orchestration de flux de travail pour la planification et la gestion des ressources, et une gestion de la mémoire pour le cache KV au-delà des limites de flux de travail. Les expériences montrent que le mappage d'étapes améliore la latence et les coûts par rapport aux baselines monomodèles.

de.title: Orla: Eine Bibliothek zum Servieren von LLM-basierten Multi-Agent-Systemen
de.summary: Orla ist eine Bibliothek zur Orchestrierung von LLM-basierten Multi-Agent-Workflows, die die manuelle Zusammensetzung von Inferenzschritten, Werkzeugaufrufen und Infrastruktur abstrahiert. Sie bietet Stufen-Mapping für die Zuweisung von Stufen zu Modellen und Back-Ends, Workflow-Orchestrierung für Planung und Ressourcenmanagement sowie Speicherverwaltung für KV-Cache über Workflow-Grenzen hinweg. Experimente zeigen, dass die Stufen-Zuordnung die Latenz und Kosten im Vergleich zu Single-Model-Baselines verbessert.

es.title: Orla: Una Biblioteca para Servir Sistemas Multi-Agente Basados en LLM
es.summary: Orla es una biblioteca para orquestar flujos de trabajo multi-agente basados en LLM que abstrae la composición manual de pasos de inferencia, llamadas de herramientas e infraestructura. Proporciona mapeo de etapas para asignar etapas a modelos y backends, orquestación de flujo de trabajo para programación y gestión de recursos, y administración de memoria para caché KV en los límites del flujo de trabajo. Los experimentos muestran que el mapeo de etapas mejora la latencia y los costos en comparación con baselines de modelo único.

title: LLM Routing as Reasoning: A MaxSAT View
summary: The paper frames LLM routing through language-conditioned preferences as a weighted MaxSAT/MaxSMT problem where natural language feedback induces constraints over model attributes. Analysis on a 25-model benchmark shows that language feedback produces near-feasible recommendation sets, while no-feedback scenarios reveal systematic priors. This constraint-based view offers a principled approach to query routing when user preferences are expressed in natural language.
content:
arXiv:2603.13612v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Routing a query through an appropriate LLM is challenging, particularly when user preferences are expressed in natural language and model attributes are only partially observable. We propose a constraint-based interpretation of language-conditioned LLM routing, formulating it as a weighted MaxSAT/MaxSMT problem in which natural language feedback induces hard and soft constraints over model attributes. Under this view, routing corresponds to selecting models that approximately maximize satisfaction of feedback-conditioned clauses. Empirical analysis on a 25-model benchmark shows that language feedback produces near-feasible recommendation sets, while no-feedback scenarios reveal systematic priors. Our results suggest that LLM routing can be understood as structured constraint optimization under language-conditioned preferences.

zh.title: LLM路由作为推理：MaxSAT视角
zh.summary: 该论文通过语言条件偏好将LLM路由框架化为加权MaxSAT/MaxSMT问题，其中自然语言反馈对模型属性施加约束。在25模型基准测试中的分析表明，语言反馈产生接近可行的推荐集，而无反馈场景揭示了系统性先验。这种基于约束的视角为用户偏好以自然语言表达时的查询路由提供了一种原则性方法。

fr.title: Routage LLM en tant que Raisonnement : Une Vue MaxSAT
fr.summary: L'article encadre le routage LLM à travers les préférences conditionnées par le langage comme un problème MaxSAT/MaxSMT pondéré où les retours en langage naturel induisent des contraintes sur les attributs du modèle. L'analyse sur un benchmark de 25 modèles montre que les retours en langage naturel produisent des ensembles de recommandation quasi réalisables, tandis que les scénarios sans retour révèlent des priors systématiques. Cette vue basée sur les contraintes offre une approche principielle au routage de requête lorsque les préférences des utilisateurs sont exprimées en langage naturel.

de.title: LLM-Routing als Reasoning: Eine MaxSAT-Perspektive
de.summary: Das Papier rahmt LLM-Routing durch sprachbedingte Präferenzen als ein gewichtetes MaxSAT/MaxSMT-Problem ein, bei dem natürlichsprachiges Feedback Einschränkungen für Modellattribute induziert. Eine Analyse auf einem 25-Modell-Benchmark zeigt, dass Sprachfeedback quasi durchführbare Empfehlungssätze erzeugt, während Szenarien ohne Feedback systematische Vorauswahlen offenbaren. Diese einschränkungsbasierte Sicht bietet einen prinzipiellen Ansatz zum Abfrage-Routing, wenn Benutzerpräferenzen in natürlicher Sprache ausgedrückt werden.

es.title: Enrutamiento LLM como Razonamiento: Una Vista MaxSAT
es.summary: El documento enmarca el enrutamiento LLM a través de preferencias condicionadas por lenguaje como un problema MaxSAT/MaxSMT ponderado donde la retroalimentación en lenguaje natural induce restricciones sobre los atributos del modelo. El análisis en un benchmark de 25 modelos muestra que la retroalimentación en lenguaje natural produce conjuntos de recomendación casi viables, mientras que los escenarios sin retroalimentación revelan priors sistemáticos. Esta vista basada en restricciones ofrece un enfoque principiado para el enrutamiento de consultas cuando las preferencias del usuario se expresan en lenguaje natural.

title: StatePlane: A Cognitive State Plane for Long-Horizon AI Systems Under Bounded Context
summary: StatePlane is a new framework that enables AI systems to maintain coherent reasoning across long interactions by managing episodic, semantic, and procedural state without expanding context windows. The approach demonstrates long-horizon intelligence is possible within existing model constraints.
content:
arXiv:2603.13644v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) and small language models (SLMs) operate under strict context window and key-value (KV) cache constraints, fundamentally limiting their ability to reason coherently over long interaction horizons. Existing approaches -- extended context windows, retrieval-augmented generation, summarization, or static documentation -- treat memory as static storage and fail to preserve decision-relevant state under long-running, multi-session tasks. We introduce StatePlane, a model-agnostic cognitive state plane that governs the formation, evolution, retrieval, and decay of episodic, semantic, and procedural state for AI systems operating under bounded context. Grounded in cognitive psychology and systems design, StatePlane formalizes episodic segmentation, selective encoding via information-theoretic constraints, goal-conditioned retrieval with intent routing, reconstructive state synthesis, and adaptive forgetting. We present a formal state model, KV-aware algorithms, security and governance mechanisms including write-path anti-poisoning, enterprise integration pathways, and an evaluation framework with six domain-specific benchmarks. StatePlane demonstrates that long-horizon intelligence can be achieved without expanding context windows or retraining models.

zh.title: StatePlane：用于长程AI系统的认知状态平面
zh.summary: StatePlane是一个新框架，能够使AI系统通过管理情节性、语义和程序性状态来在长交互过程中维持连贯的推理，而无需扩展上下文窗口。该方法展示了在现有模型约束内实现长程智能是可能的。

fr.title: StatePlane : Un plan d'état cognitif pour les systèmes IA longue portée
fr.summary: StatePlane est un nouveau cadre qui permet aux systèmes d'IA de maintenir un raisonnement cohérent sur de longues interactions en gérant l'état épisodique, sémantique et procédural sans élargir les fenêtres de contexte. L'approche démontre que l'intelligence longue portée est possible dans les contraintes du modèle existant.

de.title: StatePlane: Eine kognitive Zustandsebene für langzeitige KI-Systeme
de.summary: StatePlane ist ein neues Framework, das es KI-Systemen ermöglicht, kohärentes Denken über lange Interaktionen hinweg zu bewahren, indem es episodische, semantische und prozeduale Zustände verwaltet, ohne Kontextfenster zu erweitern. Der Ansatz zeigt, dass Langzeit-Intelligenz innerhalb der bestehenden Modellbeschränkungen möglich ist.

es.title: StatePlane: Un plano de estado cognitivo para sistemas de IA de largo horizonte
es.summary: StatePlane es un nuevo marco que permite a los sistemas de IA mantener un razonamiento coherente a través de interacciones prolongadas mediante la gestión de estados episódicos, semánticos y procedurales sin expandir ventanas de contexto. El enfoque demuestra que la inteligencia de largo horizonte es posible dentro de las limitaciones del modelo existente.

title: LLM-MINE: Large Language Model based Alzheimer's Disease and Related Dementias Phenotypes Mining from Clinical Notes
summary: LLM-MINE automatically extracts Alzheimer's disease phenotypes from unstructured clinical notes using large language models, enabling early detection and disease staging. The method substantially outperforms traditional biomedical extraction approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.13673v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Accurate extraction of Alzheimer's Disease and Related Dementias (ADRD) phenotypes from electronic health records (EHR) is critical for early-stage detection and disease staging. However, this information is usually embedded in unstructured textual data rather than tabular data, making it difficult to be extracted accurately. We therefore propose LLM-MINE, a Large Language Model-based phenotype mining framework for automatic extraction of ADRD phenotypes from clinical notes. Using two expert-defined phenotype lists, we evaluate the extracted phenotypes by examining their statistical significance across cohorts and their utility for unsupervised disease staging. Chi-square analyses confirm statistically significant phenotype differences across cohorts, with memory impairment being the strongest discriminator. Few-shot prompting with the combined phenotype lists achieves the best clustering performance (ARI=0.290, NMI=0.232), substantially outperforming biomedical NER and dictionary-based baselines. Our results demonstrate that LLM-based phenotype extraction is a promising tool for discovering clinically meaningful ADRD signals from unstructured notes.

zh.title: LLM-MINE：基于大语言模型的阿尔茨海默病表型从临床笔记中挖掘
zh.summary: LLM-MINE使用大语言模型自动从非结构化临床笔记中提取阿尔茨海默病表型，能够进行早期检测和疾病分期。该方法在性能上大大优于传统生物医学提取方法。

fr.title: LLM-MINE : Extraction de phénotypes d'Alzheimer à partir de notes cliniques
fr.summary: LLM-MINE extrait automatiquement les phénotypes de la maladie d'Alzheimer à partir de notes cliniques non structurées en utilisant les grands modèles de langage, permettant la détection précoce et l'étape de la maladie. La méthode surpasse considérablement les approches d'extraction biomédicale traditionnelles.

de.title: LLM-MINE: Extraktion von Alzheimer-Phänotypen aus klinischen Notizen
de.summary: LLM-MINE extrahiert automatisch Alzheimer-Phänotypen aus unstrukturierten klinischen Notizen unter Verwendung großer Sprachmodelle und ermöglicht frühe Erkennung und Krankheitsstadium. Die Methode übertrifft traditionelle biomedizinische Extraktionsmethoden erheblich.

es.title: LLM-MINE: Extracción de fenotipos de Alzheimer de notas clínicas
es.summary: LLM-MINE extrae automáticamente fenotipos de Alzheimer de notas clínicas no estructuradas utilizando grandes modelos de lenguaje, permitiendo la detección temprana y la estadificación de la enfermedad. El método supera sustancialmente los enfoques de extracción biomédica tradicionales.

title: TheraAgent: Multi-Agent Framework with Self-Evolving Memory and Evidence-Calibrated Reasoning for PET Theranostics
summary: TheraAgent is an AI framework for predicting treatment outcomes in PET theranostics for prostate cancer, using specialized experts and trial-grounded reasoning. It achieves 75.7% accuracy on real patient data, outperforming existing AI agents by over 20%.
content:
arXiv:2603.13676v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: PET theranostics is transforming precision oncology, yet treatment response varies substantially; many patients receiving 177Lu-PSMA radioligand therapy (RLT) for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) fail to respond, demanding reliable pre-therapy prediction. While LLM-based agents have shown remarkable potential in complex medical diagnosis, their application to PET theranostic outcome prediction remains unexplored, which faces three key challenges: (1) data and knowledge scarcity: RLT was only FDA-approved in 2022, yielding few training cases and insufficient domain knowledge in general LLMs; (2) heterogeneous information integration: robust prediction hinges on structured knowledge extraction from PET/CT, laboratory tests, and free-text clinical documentation; (3) evidence-grounded reasoning: clinical decisions must be anchored in trial evidence rather than LLM hallucinations. In this paper, we present TheraAgent, to our knowledge, the first agentic framework for PET theranostics, with three core innovations: (1) Multi-Expert Feature Extraction with Confidence-Weighted Consensus, where three specialized experts process heterogeneous inputs with uncertainty quantification; (2) Self-Evolving Agentic Memory (SEA-Mem), which learns prognostic patterns from accumulated cases, enabling case-based reasoning from limited data; (3) Evidence-Calibrated Reasoning, integrating a curated theranostics knowledge base to ground predictions in VISION/TheraP trial evidence. Evaluated on 35 real patients and 400 synthetic cases, TheraAgent achieves 75.7% overall accuracy on real patients and 87.0% on synthetic cases, outperforming MDAgents and MedAgent-Pro by over 20%. These results highlight a promising blueprint for trustworthy AI agents in PET theranostics, enabling trial-calibrated, multi-source decision support. Code will be released upon acceptance.

zh.title: TheraAgent：具有自演化记忆和证据校准推理的多代理框架用于PET放射性药物治疗学

title: InterventionLens: A Multi-Agent Framework for Detecting ASD Intervention Strategies in Parent-Child Shared Reading
summary: InterventionLens automatically detects and analyzes caregiver intervention strategies in parent-child reading sessions without task-specific training. The system achieves 79.44% accuracy in identifying autism intervention strategies, significantly outperforming baseline approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.13710v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Home-based interventions like parent-child shared reading provide a cost-effective approach for supporting children with autism spectrum disorder (ASD). However, analyzing caregiver intervention strategies in naturalistic home interactions typically relies on expert annotation, which is costly, time-intensive, and difficult to scale. To address this challenge, we propose InterventionLens, an end-to-end multi-agent system for automatically detecting and temporally segmenting caregiver intervention strategies from shared reading videos. Without task-specific model training or fine-tuning, InterventionLens uses a collaborative multi-agent architecture to integrate multimodal interaction content and perform fine-grained strategy analysis. Experiments on the ASD-HI dataset show that InterventionLens achieves an overall F1 score of 79.44\%, outperforming the baseline by 19.72\%. These results suggest that InterventionLens is a promising system for analyzing caregiver intervention strategies in home-based ASD shared reading settings. Additional resources will be released on the project page.

title: MeTok: An Efficient Meteorological Tokenization with Hyper-Aligned Group Learning for Precipitation Nowcasting
summary: MeTok introduces a distribution-centric meteorological tokenization scheme that groups similar weather features to improve precipitation nowcasting. The approach achieves 8.2% improvement in extreme precipitation prediction compared to traditional position-based methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.13752v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recently, Transformer-based architectures have advanced meteorological prediction. However, this position-centric tokenizer conflicts with the core principle of meteorological systems, where the weather phenomena undoubtedly involve synergistic interactions among multiple elements while positional information constitutes merely a component of the boundary conditions. This paper focuses primarily on the task of precipitation nowcasting and develops an efficient distribution-centric Meteorological Tokenization (MeTok) scheme, which spatially sequences to group similar meteorological features. Based on the rearrangement, realigned group learning enhances robustness across precipitation patterns, especially extreme ones. Specifically, we introduce the Hyper-Aligned Grouping Transformer (HyAGTransformer) with two key improvements: 1) The Grouping Attention (GA) mechanism uses MeTok to enable self-aligned learning of features from different precipitation patterns; 2) The Neighborhood Feed-Forward Network (N-FFN) integrates adjacent group features, aggregating contextual information to boost patch embedding discriminability. Experiments on the ERA5 dataset for 6-hour forecasts show our method improves the IoU metric by at least 8.2% in extreme precipitation prediction compared to other methods. Additionally, it gains performance with more training data and increased parameters, demonstrating scalability, stability, and superiority over traditional methods.

zh.title: MeTok：用于降水临近预报的高效气象符号化与超对齐分组学习
zh.summary: MeTok引入了一种分布中心的气象符号化方案，可将相似的天气特征分组，以改进降水临近预报。与传统的基于位置的方法相比，该方法在极端降水预报中实现了8.2%的改进。

fr.title: MeTok : Une Tokenisation Météorologique Efficace avec Apprentissage de Groupes Hyper-Alignés pour la Prévision de Précipitation
fr.summary: MeTok introduit un schéma de tokenisation météorologique centré sur la distribution qui groupe des caractéristiques météorologiques similaires pour améliorer la prévision de précipitation. L'approche atteint une amélioration de 8,2% dans la prédiction de précipitation extrême par rapport aux méthodes traditionnelles basées sur la position.

de.title: MeTok: Eine effiziente meteorologische Tokenisierung mit hyperausgerichteter Gruppenlernweise für die Niederschlagsprognose
de.summary: MeTok führt ein verteilungszentriertes meteorologisches Tokenisierungsschema ein, das ähnliche Wettereigenschaften gruppiert, um die Niederschlagsprognose zu verbessern. Der Ansatz erreicht eine 8,2%ige Verbesserung bei der Vorhersage extremer Niederschläge im Vergleich zu traditionellen positionsbasierten Methoden.

es.title: MeTok: Una Tokenización Meteorológica Eficiente con Aprendizaje de Grupos Hiper-Alineados para Pronóstico de Precipitación
es.summary: MeTok introduce un esquema de tokenización meteorológica centrado en la distribución que agrupa características climáticas similares para mejorar el pronóstico de precipitación. El enfoque logra una mejora del 8,2% en la predicción de precipitación extrema en comparación con métodos tradicionales basados en posición.

title: Multimodal Emotion Regression with Multi-Objective Optimization and VAD-Aware Audio Modeling for the 10th ABAW EMI Track
summary: A multimodal emotion prediction system combining video and audio features with multi-objective optimization competed in the ABAW emotional mimicry challenge. The approach achieved a Pearson correlation coefficient of 0.479 through feature-level concatenation and VAD-aware audio modeling.
content:
arXiv:2603.13760v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We participated in the 10th ABAW Challenge, focusing on the Emotional Mimicry Intensity (EMI) Estimation track on the Hume-Vidmimic2 dataset. This task aims to predict six continuous emotion dimensions: Admiration, Amusement, Determination, Empathic Pain, Excitement, and Joy. Through systematic multimodal exploration of pretrained high-level features, we found that, under our pretrained feature setting, direct feature concatenation outperformed the more complex fusion strategies we tested. This empirical finding motivated us to design a systematic approach built upon three core principles: (i) preserving modality-specific attributes through feature-level concatenation; (ii) improving training stability and metric alignment via multi-objective optimization; and (iii) enriching acoustic representations with a VAD-inspired latent prior. Our final framework integrates concatenation-based multimodal fusion, a shared six-dimensional regression head, multi-objective optimization with MSE, Pearson-correlation, and auxiliary branch supervision, EMA for parameter stabilization, and a VAD-inspired latent prior for the acoustic branch. On the official validation set, the proposed scheme achieved our best mean Pearson Correlation Coefficient of 0.478567.

zh.title: 多模态情感回归与多目标优化和VAD感知音频建模用于第10届ABAW EMI赛道
zh.summary: 一个多模态情感预测系统结合了视频和音频特征以及多目标优化，参与了ABAW情感模仿挑战赛。该方法通过特征级串联和VAD感知音频建模实现了0.479的皮尔逊相关系数。

El enfoque logró un coeficiente de correlación de Pearson de 0,479 mediante concatenación a nivel de características y modelado de audio consciente de VAD.

title: Artificial intelligence-driven improvement of hospital logistics management resilience: a practical exploration based on H Hospital
summary: A case study at a major hospital demonstrates AI integration significantly improves logistics resilience, with greatest gains in equipment maintenance and resource allocation. Adaptive management systems and continuous improvement processes are critical for successful AI-healthcare integration.
content:
arXiv:2603.13816v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Hospital logistics management faces growing pressure from internal operations and external emergencies, with artificial intelligence (AI) holding untapped potential to boost its resilience. This study explores AI's role in enhancing logistics resilience via a mixed-methods case study of H Hospital, combining 12 key informant interviews and a full survey of 151 logistics staff, with the PDCA cycle as the analytical framework. Thematic and quantitative analyses (hierarchical regression, structural equation modeling) were adopted for data analysis. Results showed 94.7% staff perceived AI application, with the strongest improvements in equipment maintenance (41.1%) and resource allocation (33.1%), but limited effects in emergency response (18.54%) and risk management (15.23%). AI integration positively correlated with logistics resilience (\b{eta}=0.642, p<0.001), with management system adaptability as a positive moderator (\b{eta}=0.208, p<0.01). The PDCA cycle fully mediated the AI-resilience relationship. We conclude AI effectively enhances logistics resilience, dependent on adaptive management systems and structured continuous improvement mechanisms. Targeted strategies are proposed to form an AI-driven closed-loop resilience mechanism, offering empirical guidance for AI-hospital logistics integration and resilient health system construction.

zh.title: 基于H医院的人工智能驱动的医院物流管理韧性改善：一项实际探索
zh.summary: 一家大型医院的案例研究表明，人工智能集成显著改善了物流韧性，在设备维护和资源配置方面获得最大收益。自适应管理系统和持续改进过程对成功的人工智能-医疗保健集成至关重要。

fr.title: Amélioration de la résilience de la gestion logistique hospitalière basée sur l'IA : une exploration pratique basée sur l'Hôpital H
fr.summary: Une étude de cas dans un grand hôpital montre que l'intégration de l'IA améliore considérablement la résilience logistique, avec les plus grands gains dans la maintenance des équipements et l'allocation des ressources. Les systèmes de gestion adaptative et les processus d'amélioration continue sont essentiels pour une intégration réussie de l'IA dans la santé.

de.title: KI-gesteuerte Verbesserung der Widerstandsfähigkeit der Krankenhauslogistik: eine praktische Erkundung basierend auf dem H-Krankenhaus

Adaptive Managementsysteme und kontinuierliche Verbesserungsprozesse sind entscheidend für eine erfolgreiche KI-Healthcare-Integration.

es.title: Mejora impulsada por IA de la resiliencia de la gestión logística hospitalaria: una exploración práctica basada en el Hospital H

Los sistemas de gestión adaptativa y los procesos de mejora continua son críticos para una integración exitosa de IA-atención médica.

title: PA-Net: Precipitation-Adaptive Mixture-of-Experts for Long-Tail Rainfall Nowcasting
summary: PA-Net dynamically allocates computational resources based on rainfall intensity to improve extreme precipitation prediction while managing massive spatiotemporal token volumes. The framework achieves consistent improvements on heavy-rain events that constitute less than 0.1% of training samples.
content:
arXiv:2603.13818v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Precipitation nowcasting is vital for flood warning, agricultural management, and emergency response, yet two bottlenecks persist: the prohibitive cost of modeling million-scale spatiotemporal tokens from multi-variate atmospheric fields, and the extreme long-tailed rainfall distribution where heavy-to-torrential events -- those of greatest societal impact -- constitute fewer than 0.1% of all samples. We propose the Precipitation-Adaptive Network (PA-Net), a Transformer framework whose computational budget is explicitly governed by rainfall intensity. Its core component, Precipitation-Adaptive MoE (PA-MoE), dynamically scales the number of activated experts per token according to local precipitation magnitude, channeling richer representational capacity toward the rare yet critical heavy-rainfall tail. A Dual-Axis Compressed Latent Attention mechanism factorizes spatiotemporal attention with convolutional reduction to manage massive context lengths, while an intensity-aware training protocol progressively amplifies learning signals from extreme-rainfall samples. Experiment on ERA5 demonstrate consistent improvements over state-of-the-art baselines, with particularly significant gains in heavy-rain and rainstorm regimes.

zh.title: PA-Net：用于长尾降雨临近预报的降水自适应混合专家
zh.summary: PA-Net根据降雨强度动态分配计算资源，以改进极端降水预报，同时管理大量时空令牌。该框架在占训练样本不足0.1%的强降雨事件上实现了持续改进。

fr.title: PA-Net : Mélange d'experts adaptatif aux précipitations pour la prévision à court terme des précipitations à longue traîne
fr.summary: PA-Net alloue dynamiquement les ressources informatiques en fonction de l'intensité des précipitations pour améliorer la prédiction des précipitations extrêmes tout en gérant d'énormes volumes de jetons spatiotemporels. Le cadre réalise des améliorations constantes sur les événements de fortes pluies qui constituent moins de 0,1% des échantillons d'entraînement.

de.title: PA-Net: Niederschlagsadaptive Mischung von Experten für Regenvorhersage mit langer Aufteilung
de.summary: PA-Net verteilt Rechenressourcen dynamisch basierend auf der Regenintensität, um die Vorhersage extremer Niederschläge zu verbessern und gleichzeitig massive spatiotemporale Token-Volumen zu bewältigen. Das Framework erreicht konsistente Verbesserungen bei Starkregenereignissen, die weniger als 0,1% der Trainingsmuster ausmachen.

es.title: PA-Net: Mezcla Adaptativa a Precipitación de Expertos para Predicción de Lluvia de Cola Larga
